title: Competitor EDA: Data-to-Action Refactor
purpose: Transform the Competitor EDA page from a raw-data dump into an operator-facing insight surface. Move from passive observation to a drill-down, human-readable analytics experience that informs posting decisions.
design_intent: SaaS-style progressive disclosure: KPI summary at top, interactive summary table in the middle, charts that respond to selection, bot signals hidden behind an advanced toggle. One screen, one primary decision: 'who is dominant in my space right now, and when should I post?'
change_impact: Modifies src/AnalyticsPage.tsx (full restructure), lib/competitorAnalytics.ts (data contract additions), and the AuthorSummaryRow type. No changes to scheduler, bot.ts, or publisher flow.

additions.description: Count of distinct calendar days on which the author published at least one post in the query range.
additions.why: Fixes postsPerDay dilution bug: dividing by calendar range instead of active days produces artificially low rates for authors who started posting recently.

additions.description: postsInRange / activeDays, rounded to 1 decimal place. Primary display metric.
additions.why: 1.1 posts/active-day is accurate and readable; 1.097 posts/calendar-day is misleading and unformatted.

additions.description: Math.round(totalViews / postsInRange). Preferred display over totalViews sum.
additions.why: Total views are skewed by author tenure. Avg views/post measures content quality independent of posting volume.

AuthorSummaryRow.note: postsPerDay (calendar-based) is retained for backward compatibility but should not be the primary display column.

AuthorBotSignals.note: Raw heuristic fields (interArrivalCv, hourEntropy, etc.) stay as-is. UI hides them behind a disclosure toggle; only heuristicTier is shown in the main table.

focusAuthor_query_param.description: Optional `focusAuthor` query param on GET /api/analytics/competitors. When present, heatmap cells are filtered to that author's events only. timeSeries still shows all authors.
focusAuthor_query_param.why: Enables the drill-down pattern where clicking a row in the summary table refocuses the heatmap without a full page reload.

hierarchy.position: inline at page top-right, below header
hierarchy.style: Small, horizontal, no bounding box. Matches Vercel/PostHog inline filter pattern.

components: KpiCard: Market Posts/Day (average across all authors in range)
KpiCard: Data Freshness (medianGapHours formatted as '18 min' or '2.4 h')
hierarchy.style: 3-column grid of stat cards. Orange accent on values. No chart in this zone.

hierarchy.interactivity: Clicking a row sets selectedAuthor state. Selected row highlighted. 'All' button deselects.
hierarchy.bot_risk_display: Colored badge only: green=Low, amber=Medium, red=High. No raw floats.
hierarchy.style: Clean table, no overflow scroll unless >10 rows.

hierarchy.behavior: Charts filter to selectedAuthor when one is selected; show all authors (top 12) when none selected.

charts.description: New posts over time. When author selected: single line for that author. When All: _total line only (no spaghetti).
charts.why: Eliminates the 12-color spaghetti problem. User gets context first, then drills.

charts.description: Share of posts per bucket. Only shown in All mode (no author selected). Hidden when single author selected to avoid redundancy.
charts.why: Share-of-voice view is a market-level lens, not an author-level lens.

charts.description: When author selected: filtered to that author's events. When All: aggregate of all events.
charts.why: Author-specific heatmap reveals their posting schedule pattern — actionable for timing competitive posts.

hierarchy.content: Full existing bot signals table with raw heuristic columns (CV, entropy, uniformity, burst)
hierarchy.style: Amber border retained to signal 'experimental' nature. Disclaimer text kept.

human_translation_rules.secondary: If activeDays >= 7, also show weekly equivalent: '~7 posts/week'
Total views shown as tooltip or footnote only
largeGapWarning shown as amber dot on freshness card
Raw signals only in zone 5 advanced section

acceptance_criteria: postsPerActiveDay is computed as postsInRange / activeDays (not calendar range) and displayed rounded to 1 decimal
Selecting an author row in the summary table filters the heatmap and timeline chart to that author's events
When no author is selected, the timeline shows only the _total line (no per-author spaghetti)
Bot signals table is collapsed by default; raw heuristic floats are not visible without expanding
KPI row shows most active competitor, market avg posts/day, and data freshness on every load
Filters are inline controls (no large bordered section block)
avgViewsPerPost is shown in the summary table; totalViews sum is not a primary column
medianGapHours < 1 is displayed in minutes, >= 1 in hours with 1 decimal
lint passes with no new TypeScript errors
No changes to scheduler, bot.ts, publisher flow, or any non-analytics API endpoints
out_of_scope: Scheduler integration with competitor trend data
Pagination or virtualization for large author lists
